findings.effort: A column in stops.txt; your scheduling software likely has it.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
findings.what: 634 of 634 stops don't say whether a wheelchair user can board there.
findings.why: Riders who use wheelchairs can't plan a trip when accessibility is marked 'unknown'; apps show no information at all.

findings.effort: Often one default setting in your export.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_accessible on every trip (most small-agency fleets are 100% accessible, so this is often a single default).
findings.what: 327 of 327 trips don't say whether the vehicle is wheelchair accessible.
findings.why: Even with accessible stops, riders need to know the bus itself can take them.

findings.effort: A small file for most flat-fare systems.
findings.fix: Add fare_attributes.txt (or Fares v2 files) with your fare structure.
findings.what: The feed contains no fare information.
findings.why: Riders see 'fare unknown' in trip planners and can't budget their trip; visitors are most affected.

findings.effort: Often a bulk fix in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Rename stops to mixed case (e.g. 'Main St & 2nd Ave').
findings.what: About 530 stop names are written in ALL CAPS.
findings.why: Mixed-case names are easier to read in apps and are read more naturally by screen readers.

findings.effort: Usually a bulk edit in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Populate trip_headsign to match what the bus displays.
findings.what: 327 of 327 trips have no headsign.
findings.why: Riders at the stop can't tell which direction a bus is going.

findings.effort: One field.
findings.fix: Add feed_contact_email to feed_info.txt.
findings.what: feed_info.txt has no technical contact (feed_contact_email or feed_contact_url).
findings.why: App makers and state data programs have nobody to email when they spot a problem with your feed, so problems linger.

completeness.name: completeness
completeness.summary: 0% of stops state wheelchair accessibility (0% marked accessible). Fare data is not published.

findings.effort: Varies.
findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'stop_time_with_arrival_before_previous_departure_time' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Stop time with arrival before previous departure time (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: A few minutes per flagged stop.
findings.fix: Check the flagged stops' coordinates and the route shape in your scheduling software; re-snap whichever is misplaced.
findings.what: Some stops sit far from the route line they belong to.
findings.why: Trip planners may draw the bus route through the wrong streets or point riders to the wrong corner.

findings.effort: Often a bulk fix in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Use mixed case for stop names and headsigns (e.g. 'Main St & 2nd Ave', not 'MAIN ST & 2ND AVE').
findings.what: Some rider-facing names are in ALL CAPS or all lowercase.
findings.why: ALL-CAPS stop and headsign names are harder to read in apps and are read awkwardly by screen readers.

findings.effort: A review pass in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Remove retired stops from the export, or add them back to the trips that should serve them.
findings.what: Some stops exist in the feed but no trip ever stops at them.
findings.why: Riders may walk to a stop where no bus is scheduled to arrive.

findings.effort: A field at a time; not urgent.
findings.fix: Review the flagged fields and fill in the ones your riders would use.
findings.what: Some files are missing recommended (not required) fields.
findings.why: Recommended fields like agency_phone or stop descriptions make the feed more useful to riders and trip planners.

findings.effort: One field per route.
findings.fix: Give each route a distinct short or long name.
findings.what: Two or more routes share the same name.
findings.why: Riders can't tell the routes apart in apps.

findings.effort: Worth a vendor question if it appears often.
findings.fix: Check the flagged trips in your scheduling software; remove them or restore their missing stops.
findings.what: Some trips serve fewer than two stops.
findings.why: A trip with one stop can't be ridden; planners ignore it and it may signal an export problem.

findings.effort: Varies.
findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'stops_match_shape_out_of_order' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Stops match shape out of order (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: Varies.
findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'unused_trip' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Unused trip (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: One setting.
findings.fix: Enable 'remove unused shapes' (or similar) in your export tool.
findings.what: The feed contains route shapes no trip uses.
findings.why: Harmless to riders, but it bloats the feed and suggests stale export data.

findings.effort: One small file, set once in export settings.
findings.fix: Add feed_info.txt with publisher name, URL, language, and feed_start_date/feed_end_date.
findings.what: A recommended GTFS file (usually feed_info.txt) is missing.
findings.why: feed_info.txt tells apps who publishes the feed and when it expires; without it nobody is warned before data goes stale.

findings.effort: A few minutes in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Delete the empty calendars or set their service days.
findings.what: Some service calendars have no days of the week switched on.
findings.why: Trips tied to these calendars never run; they are dead data that can mask real schedule problems.

findings.effort: Varies.
findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'service_extends_far_in_the_future' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Service extends far in the future (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

correctness.name: correctness
correctness.summary: The MobilityData validator flagged 13 kinds of issues: 69 error, 1629 warning, and 27 informational instances in total.

findings.effort: Two fields, set once in export settings.
findings.fix: Add feed_info.txt with feed_start_date and feed_end_date to your export.
findings.what: feed_info.txt is missing its start/end dates (the file itself is absent)
findings.why: Apps and this scorecard can't warn anyone before the feed goes stale without stated validity dates.

freshness.name: freshness
freshness.summary: Service data covers the next 3482 days.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.summary: Not scored yet. Nothing here counts against the grade.

feed.license_note: No stated data license in the Mobility Database; verify before publishing.

top_fixes.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'stop_time_with_arrival_before_previous_departure_time' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
top_fixes.what: Stop time with arrival before previous departure time (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
top_fixes.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

top_fixes.effort: A column in stops.txt; your scheduling software likely has it.
top_fixes.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
top_fixes.what: 634 of 634 stops don't say whether a wheelchair user can board there.
top_fixes.why: Riders who use wheelchairs can't plan a trip when accessibility is marked 'unknown'; apps show no information at all.

top_fixes.effort: Often one default setting in your export.
top_fixes.fix: Set wheelchair_accessible on every trip (most small-agency fleets are 100% accessible, so this is often a single default).
top_fixes.what: 327 of 327 trips don't say whether the vehicle is wheelchair accessible.
top_fixes.why: Even with accessible stops, riders need to know the bus itself can take them.
